english: gain, profit, k'e-spógs (khe spogs) id.; spogs byéd-pa (spogs byed pa) to make profit, to gain money; tsoṅ-spógs byéd-pa (tshong spogs byed pa) to gain money by traffic Dzl.; tsoṅ-spógs-la _gró-ba (tshong spogs la 'gro ba) Dzl.; skyed-spogs (skyed spogs) interest (of money); spógs-su gtóṅ-ba (spogs su gtong ba) to give money on interest Cs.; *mi-póg lém-pa* (*mi pog lem pa*) C. to demand a tax from emigrants or travellers.

pf. spaṅs (spangs), fut. spaṅ (spang), imp. spoṅ(s) (spong(s)), (Ssk. प्रहा) 1. to give up, to declare off, bdag daṅ bdag-gir (bdag dang bdag gir) Sambh. to give one's self up and all that one has; sman-dpyád mi byéd-par spóṅ-na (sman dpyad mi byed par spong na) if he gives (the patient) over without even attempting a cure Dzl. ༥༠, ༡; to renounce (all pleasures) frq.; *kód-gu-ru spaṅ mi pód* (*kod gu ru spang mi pod*) he cannot give up Kotgur (his former residence) or forget it; without an object: yóṅs-su spóṅ-ba (yongs su spong ba) (partic.) they (the Bodhisattvas) who entirely renounce Thgy.; to shun, avoid, abstain from (faults, sins, certain food) frq.; to reject = _dór-ba ('dor ba): bde-sdúg-la spaṅ-blaṅ méd (bde sdug la spang blang med) between happiness and unhappiness there was no need to choose (sc. because only bliss prevailed) Glr.; spoṅ-blaṅ _dzín-pa źíg-pa (spong blang 'dzin pa zhig pa) the cessation of every inclination and disinclination, or also, of every interest in choosing or rejecting. - 2. to throw off, to drop, a letter, pyi-tség (phyi tsheg) (to omit) the dot after a syllable Gram.
Vaisali, ancient town near Allahabad, Tar. 7, 5 and elsewh.; also Vriji, acc. to Schf.
spice Med.; spod _débs-pa (spod 'debs pa) to season; spód-čan (spod can) seasoned.
1. hermit, spód-k'aṅ (spod khang) hermitage Sch. - 2. vow, spód-po nyáms-pa (spod po nyams pa) one that has broken his vow Sch.

(W. also *spós-pa* (*spos pa*)), 1. vb. to dare, to venture, jú-bar mi spóbs-pas ('ju bar mi spobs pas) not daring to take hold of Pth., also Dzl. ༢༤, ༤; ༣༢, ༡༦; spóbs-par byéd-pa (spobs par byed pa) 1. id. 2. to enable, empower, authorize Cs. - 2. sbst. courage, confidence.
diffuse (in words), prolix, long-winded, byéd-pa (byed pa), smrá-ba (smra ba), čád-pa (chad pa) Cs. 'to say circumstantially'.
, spo-ré (spo re), steel-yard; W. particularly a little one.

pf. and fut. spar (spar), 1. to lift up, rdó-rje (rdo rje) the praying-sceptre Dom.; (a hatchet) to fetch a blow; W. *śed spár-la* (*shed spar la*) (or *spár-te* (*spar te*)) *rgyob* (*rgyob*) swing (the hatchet) well and strike! *spár-la čoṅ* (*spar la chong*) run and leap! cf. also nems (nems); to raise, promote, advance, go-páṅ (go phang) in rank Lex. - 2. v. dpór-ba (dpor ba).
Ts. for *me-mé* (*me me*) W. (v. mes-po (mes po)).

1. sbst. incense; bdug-spós (bdug spos) id.; less frq. perfume in general; byug-spós (byug spos) sweet-scented water or ointment; spos sbyór-bu (spos sbyor bu), sgrúb-pa (sgrub pa), also rgyáb-pa (rgyab pa) and rgyág-pa (rgyag pa) Cs., to prepare incense, perfumes, bdúg-pa (bdug pa) to burn (incense); _byúg-pa ('byug pa) to cover (with perfume); rgya-spós (rgya spos), bzaṅ-spós (bzang spos), spaṅ-spós (spang spos), different kinds of perfume; spos-(kyi) réṅ(-bu) (spos (kyi) reng (bu)) pastil, long and thin straws being covered with an odoriferous substance, which generally consists of pulverized śugpa (shug pa), and sandal-wood, combined with some gugul (gu gul), musk and the like; they are made by the Lamas, and frequently presented to travellers as an offering of welcome. spos-dkár (spos dkar) frankincense, = gugúl dkár-po (gu gul dkar po). - spós-mk'an (spos mkhan) perfumer. - spos-čág (spos chag) incense in pieces or cakes. - spos-čú (spos chu), resp. čab (chab), sweet-scented water, diluted ointment, lús-la _byúg-pa (lus la 'byug pa) Pth.; spós-čus čag-čág _débs-pa (spos chus chag chag 'debs pa) Pth. to sprinkle with such water. - spos-snód (spos snod) Cs., spos-pór (spos por) (also pog-pór (pog por)), censer, perfuming-pan. - spós-tsoṅ-pa (spos tshong pa) = spós-mk'an (spos mkhan). - spos-gźóṅ (spos gzhong) basin for incense Cs. - spos-śél (spos shel) (col. *po-śél* (*po shel*)) amber. - 2. vb. v. spó-ba (spo ba) and spóbs-pa (spobs pa).
Cs. = yo-byád (yo byad); Lexx. spyad-dṅós (spyad dngos) and dṅos-spyád (dngos spyad), as explanations to ka-ča (ka ca).

Mil., Sg., -gi (gi) Dzl., -ku (-ku), -gu (-gu), ku (ku) Cs., Lh. *śaṅ-ku* (*shang ku*), wolf. (Wolves, where more frequent, as e.g. in Spiti, commit ravages among the sheep; but are other wise not much dreaded by man). spyaṅ-mo (spyang mo) female wolf; spyaṅ-prúg (spyang phrug) young wolf; spyaṅ-tsáṅ (spyang tshang) wolf's den; spyaṅ-dóṅ (spyang dong) wolf's trap (used in Sp.); spyaṅ-ku ṅú-ba (spyang ku ngu ba) the howling of a wolf Cs.; če-spyaṅ (ce spyang) Lex.,
